pages.notes: Index page listing page numbers 38–60 with corresponding place names. Entry 40 'Noux' may be a place name or possibly 'Noues' — unclear. Entry 56 partially obscured — reads '[?Pierre] Marie sur Ouche'; the first part of the saint's name is hard to make out, possibly 'St. Pierre' or similar. Entry 57 appears twice: first with 'Dijon' struck through and 'Canal to R....' (rest cut off or illegible), then again as '57 Barbirey Gardens / Dijon'. The repeated 57 may be a correction. 'Charinghill' at entry 48 could read 'Charing Hill' or 'Charring Hill' — transcribed as written.

pages.full_text:
Thursday July 31st 2008            Joan swimming pool
                                   Charles says ok **
We slept in __Zamora campsite__ + drove into
Zamora the next day, parking across the
river. The Cathedral has an amazing, fragile-
looking dome. Simon, Eve & Charles (19) are in
Spain. We arrived here by ferry with Thomas, Nadin
+ Austin who have gone over the Pyrenees in their
green camping van. We are looking at
Wellington's Battle Fields before joining them at
David's house.
   The tapestries in the cathedral museum
were fresh-looking with bright colours. The
misericords had been nailed down by a
bashful bishop because they showed lewd
monks. The present bishop made sure we
didn't get to see them, either, by roping
them off.
photo_captions: VISITA A LA S.I. CATEDRAL DE ZAMORA Y MUSEO
pages.notes: Glued-in ticket at bottom of page: 'VISITA A LA S.I. CATEDRAL DE ZAMORA Y MUSEO' (two overlapping ticket stubs, one teal/blue, one yellow). Ticket shows 'Importe: 3,00 €' and 'N° 177636'. Marginal note top right reads 'Joan swimming pool / Charles says ok **' — appears to be a separate annotation. 'Nadin' may be short for 'Nadine' — transcribed as written. 'Wellington's Battle Fields' likely refers to the battlefield at or near Salamanca/Ciudad Rodrigo area.
